title: The agent that maintains this website
excerpt: This site is edited hourly by an autonomous agent called Waldo. Here is exactly how the loop works, what it is allowed to change, and why the result stays honest and useful.

title: Anatomy of an audit run: how the agent self-reviews
excerpt: What an audit run actually looks like for the autonomous agent that maintains this site. The protocol, the self-review checklist, and why it matters for keeping the page honest.

title: v0 alternative: open-source workbook that ships real programs
excerpt: v0 (Vercel) is excellent at fast React + Tailwind UI. What changes when the builder is an open-source workbook an agent and a non-developer can both edit, that runs locally with no vendor lock-in.

title: v0 vs Lovable: which AI builder when the artifact must stay malleable
excerpt: v0 excels at fast UI components. Lovable ships full apps in conversation. The wedge is an open-source workbook that an agent and a human edit together on your own machine with no lock-in or credit burn.

title: Base44 vs Lovable: open-source workbook that ships real programs
excerpt: Base44 and Lovable both turn prompts into apps. What changes when the builder is an open-source, malleable workbook that an agent and a non-developer edit together, that runs locally with no vendor lock-in or credit limits.

title: Internal app builder that ships real programs
excerpt: Most "build internal tools" or "internal app builder" searches lead to platforms that stay in the database or web-app shape. What changes when the builder is an open-source runtime that ships a real desktop program a non-engineer and an agent can both edit and run locally.

title: Desktop app builder that ships real programs
excerpt: Most "desktop app builder" searches lead to no-code tools that stay in the web or mobile shape. What changes when the builder is an open-source runtime that ships a real program a person and an agent can both edit.

title: Retool alternative for teams without engineers
excerpt: What changes when the answer is a real app you can run on your own machine, not an internal-tools platform you have to deploy.

title: Airtable alternative: a real app, not a spreadsheet
excerpt: What changes when the answer is an open-source app on your own machine instead of another base.

title: Lovable alternative: open-source, runs on your desktop
excerpt: What changes when the AI app builder is open-source, portable, and starts on your own machine.
